title: Transcription start sites from capped small RNA-seq of rat nucleus accumbens and prefrontal cortex
author: Francesca, Telese
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Small RNAs of ∼15–60 nt were size selected by denaturing gel electrophoresis starting from total RNA extracted from 14 rat brain tissue dissections. For csRNA libraries, cap selection was followed by decapping, adapter ligation, and sequencing. For input libraries, 10% of small RNA input was used for decapping, adapter ligation, and sequencing. After library quality check by gel electrophoresis, the samples were sequenced using the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using 75 cycles single end. Sequencing reads were aligned to the rat mRatBN7.2 genome assembly using STAR v2.5.3a aligner with default parameters. Transcriptional start regions were defined using HOMER’s findPeaks tool. Duttke, S.H., Montilla-Perez, P., Chang, M.W., Li, H., Chen, H., Carrette, L.L.G., de Guglielmo, G., George, O., Palmer, A.A., Benner, C., et al. (2022). Glucocorticoid Receptor-Regulated Enhancers Play a Central Role in the Gene Regulatory Networks Underlying Drug Addiction. Front. Neurosci. 16, 858427.
